MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day, January 27, 2026 at 3:30 pm for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Kelly-Moore Paint Company, Inc. and K-M Industries Holding Co., Inc.

Dept. 304. Judge: Ethan P. Schulman. Clerk: Edward Santos, Reporter: NOT RECORDED (FTR). (304)

No Appearance by any party or counsel. Not contested.

After considering the written evidence presented and good cause appearing, the court adopts it's tentative ruling in favor of the Plaintiffs as follows:

The Application of Plaintiffs Margarita Kangas, as Successor-in-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of Wayne Kangas, Deceased, and Annalisse de Vroome, Aili Lehrfeld, and Stephanie Krizman, as Wrongful Death Heirs of Wayne Kangas, Deceased, for Entry of Default Against Defendants: (1) Kelly-Moore Paint Company, Inc., in the amount of $2,453,699.00; and (2) K-M Industries Holding Co., Inc., in the amount of $2,453,699.00 is GRANTED.

The Court has reviewed the prove-up packet including the Judicial Council Form CIV-100, the memorandum, Mr. Nevin's declaration and the attached exhibits, which are admitted. The Court finds Defendants have been served with process, failed to answer or appear and defend the action within the time allowed by law, and default has been duly entered. As such, Plaintiffs are entitled to the relief sought in the Application. The Court intends to execute the proposed judgment accompanying the application.

RULING:
Not argued, not contested, and the tentative ruling is adopted: Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 36(a). No opposition filed. The trial date is December 11, 2023 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is December 22, 2023. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is December 7, 2023. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation.
Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is November 24, 2023. Expert discovery cut-off date is December 8, 2023. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than September 21, 2023. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission.
